expected_findings.description: resolve_and_validate_host receives a full URL (e.g., http://127.0.0.1) via merged_config['endpoint_url'], but passes it directly to socket.getaddrinfo(host, None). getaddrinfo expects a hostname, not a URL, so internal IP detection would fail because getaddrinfo cannot resolve a URL string, enabling SSRF bypass.

The Postgres/Redshift validation passes a bare host (not a URL) to resolve_and_validate_host, but there are no tests confirming this works correctly. Passing a host rather than a URL to the validation function needs explicit test coverage to prevent regressions.

The runtime check for aws_access_key_id and aws_secret_access_key may appear redundant given the type annotations now declare them as non-optional str, but it serves as good defensive programming to catch empty strings that would pass type checking.
